title: Lithocarpus maingayi
taxonomy: Lithocarpus maingayi is classified under the family Fagaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as beech and oak trees. It belongs to the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of evergreen trees native to Asia. The species L. maingayi is native to Malaysia and is also known as the Malaysian beech. It is a species of evergreen tree that can grow up to 40 meters tall. The tree is characterized by its smooth, gray bark and its elliptical leaves.
uses: Lithocarpus maingayi is a species of evergreen tree that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. The bark and leaves of the tree have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In traditional medicine, the bark is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and pain. Additionally, the leaves are believed to have antiseptic properties, which may help to prevent infection. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lithocarpus maingayi in modern medicine.

constituents: Lithocarpus maingayi, also known as the Malayan tanaw or Beccari's tanaw, is a species of evergreen tree. The active constituents of Lithocarpus maingayi have not been extensively documented, but studies suggest that it contains various bioactive compounds. The Malaysian stone oak is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
